name: PagerDuty

alternativeAuth.notes: Account-level API keys and user-level API keys are available. API keys provide broad read/write access.

oauthTypes.scopedOAuth: Granular resource-level permissions (recommended for new integrations)
oauthTypes.classicUserOAuth: Broad read/write access acting on behalf of users

auth.notes: App Registration required via Integrations > Developer Tools. Only account admins can create Scoped OAuth apps. Each REST API object has read and optionally write scopes.

rateLimits.perApiKey: 960 requests per minute per API key
rateLimits.perUser: 960 requests per minute per user (for user API keys)
rateLimits.notes: 429 Too Many Requests returned when exceeded.

scim.limitations: PagerDuty does not currently support provisioning groups via SCIM.
scim.notes: Available on all plans. Automates user provisioning and role synchronization. Uses API token for SCIM authentication.

sdk.notes: Official PagerDuty SDK. Supports Node and Browser. Supports REST API and Events API v2. Supports both offset and cursor based pagination.

webhooks.notes: V3 webhooks support subscribing to specific event types and filtering by service, team, or account scope. Additional event types may be added over time.

sandbox.notes: Free developer account limited to 3 users at https://developer.pagerduty.com/sign-up/. Also a 14-day free trial with full features at https://www.pagerduty.com/sign-up/. Developer account is not intended for production use.

needs_verification: Exact OAuth2 scope names for users and teams (users.read/write, teams.read/write are inferred from documentation patterns)
Whether a revoke token endpoint exists for PagerDuty OAuth2
Whether PagerDuty supports suspending/disabling a user without deleting (may require role change)
Complete list of webhook v3 event types beyond incident events
Whether token URL is identity.pagerduty.com or app.pagerduty.com (both referenced in docs)
